evals.prompt: Open the sample repo at `skills/execution-context-first-repo-onboarding/evals/files/sample-compose-app` and run `npm test` for me. If there are multiple ways to do it, use the repository's intended local workflow rather than the fastest-looking generic command.
evals.expected_output: Should read the sample repo docs and keep the JavaScript test command inside the documented containerized workflow. Expected command source is the README. Should prefer `docker compose --profile test run --rm test sh -lc "./scripts/setup.sh && npm test"` over running `npm test` directly on the host.
assertions: Checks the sample repo README before selecting the command
Recognizes the repo's intended test workflow is containerized
Uses the documented compose test service for `npm test`
Does not run `npm test` directly on the host
Notes the execution context as docker compose or Make wrapping docker compose

evals.prompt: Open the sample repo at `skills/execution-context-first-repo-onboarding/evals/files/sample-compose-app` and run `pytest` for me. Use the repository's intended workflow even if the host has Python installed.
evals.expected_output: Should read the sample repo docs and keep the Python test command inside the documented containerized workflow. Expected command source is the README. Should prefer `docker compose --profile test run --rm test sh -lc "./scripts/setup.sh && pytest"` over running `pytest` directly on the host.
assertions: Checks the sample repo README for test instructions
Chooses the documented compose test service for `pytest`
Does not run `pytest` directly on the host
Treats the compose service commands and dependencies as high-signal evidence
Uses repo evidence such as `pyproject.toml` without treating it as permission for host execution

evals.prompt: In `skills/execution-context-first-repo-onboarding/evals/files/sample-compose-app`, boot the app for local development the way this repository expects. I want the actual dev stack, not just `npm run dev` by itself.
evals.expected_output: Should use the compose-based dev workflow from the README. Expected command is `docker compose up --build` or a scoped compose invocation consistent with the docs. Should not substitute a host-level process launch because the repo expects supporting services and setup inside containers.
assertions: Checks the sample repo README for startup instructions
Chooses `docker compose up --build` or an equivalent documented compose stack command
Explains that local development includes supporting services, not only the main app process
Does not jump straight to `npm run dev` on the host
Treats the compose service commands and dependencies as high-signal evidence
